pkg/framework/entutils/testutils.purpose: Organisational parent for two isolated Ent codegen fixture databases (ent1, ent2) used exclusively to test pkg/framework/entutils transaction helpers (TransactingRepo, HijackTx, savepoints) without touching production openmeter/ent/db. Each child is self-contained: schema -> codegen -> generated db/ client, with its own package path scoped under testutils.

patterns.name: Asymmetric extension sets between fixtures
patterns.description: ent1 registers all three framework extensions (entcursor, entexpose, entpaginate); ent2 registers only entexpose. The asymmetry models a minimal second-DB fixture so cross-DB transaction tests work without cursor/paginate overhead on the second client.
patterns.example: ent1/entc.go: entc.Extensions(entcursor.New(), entexpose.New(), entpaginate.New()) vs ent2/entc.go: entc.Extensions(entexpose.New())

patterns.name: entexpose required on both fixtures
patterns.description: Both fixtures must include entexpose.New() so the generated db/ exposes GetConfig(), HijackTx(), NewTxClientFromRawConfig(), and ExposedTxDriver — the hooks TransactingRepo uses to rebind ctx-carried transactions to a typed client.
patterns.example: ent1/db/expose.go: func (c *Client) HijackTx(...) and func NewTxClientFromRawConfig(...)

patterns.name: Ignored-main codegen driver
patterns.description: entc.go carries //go:build ignore so it is excluded from normal compilation; generate.go in the parent holds the //go:generate go run -mod=mod entc.go directive.

patterns.name: Schema uses entutils.TimeMixin
patterns.description: Both fixture schemas embed entutils.TimeMixin{} in Mixin(), mirroring production shared-mixin usage. Any new test schema must include it.

patterns.name: Package path scoped under testutils
patterns.description: entc.go Package must resolve to the correct testutils sub-path; a wrong path overwrites the wrong generated package.

patterns.name: Standard four Ent features on both fixtures
patterns.description: Both entc.go files declare the same four gen.Feature values; removing any diverges test clients from production and breaks transaction-level assertions.

key_file_guides.role: Codegen driver for ent1; registers all three framework extensions and all four features. Run via go generate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add production domain imports; never point Target/Package at production paths.

key_file_guides.role: Codegen driver for ent2; intentionally minimal — only entexpose.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add entcursor or entpaginate — ent2 is the minimal second-DB fixture by design.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by entexpose; exposes HijackTx, NewTxClientFromRawConfig, ExposedTxDriver. DO NOT EDIT.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Primary integration surface for TransactingRepo savepoint tests; changes require regeneration, not hand-edits.

key_file_guides.role: Generated helper opening a db.Client and auto-running migration via migrate.Tables; used to bootstrap a fresh test-postgres DB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT; regenerate. enttest.Open/NewClient auto-migrate using ent1 migrate.Tables.

key_file_guides.role: Minimal fixture schema with TimeMixin, string id, one value field; source of truth for ent1 codegen.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep minimal; do not import production domain packages.

key_file_guides.role: Parallel minimal fixture schema for the ent2 second-DB client.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep minimal; do not share schema types with ent1 — they represent independent DBs.

anti_patterns: Pointing Target or Package in entc.go at production openmeter/ent/db paths — overwrites production generated code.
Adding entcursor or entpaginate extensions to ent2 — destroys the intentional asymmetry and fattens the minimal fixture.
Removing any of the four standard gen.Feature flags from either fixture — diverges the test client from production capabilities.
Importing production domain packages (openmeter/billing, openmeter/customer, etc.) into schema/ or entc.go.
Hand-editing any file under ent1/db/ or ent2/db/ — all are generated; re-run go generate instead.

decisions.decision: Two separate fixture databases (ent1 + ent2) instead of one.
decisions.rationale: TransactingRepo and HijackTx tests must prove cross-DB transaction propagation; a single fixture cannot exercise the multi-client path production code uses when queries span DB boundaries.

decisions.decision: ent1 carries all three framework extensions; ent2 carries only entexpose.
decisions.rationale: ent1 exercises the full framework surface (cursor, paginate, expose); ent2 is intentionally minimal to isolate second-client transaction behavior.

decisions.decision: Fixtures live under pkg/framework/entutils/testutils, not under openmeter/*/testutils.
decisions.rationale: The tested code is in pkg/framework/entutils; co-locating fixtures avoids import cycles with domain packages and keeps scaffolding next to the framework it validates.

code_examples.scenario: How entexpose's HijackTx is used by TransactingRepo to rebind a ctx-carried transaction to a typed ent1 client
